description: A three-pattern 1930s Delta blues in A minor. Full 12-bar I-IV-V form with deterministic walking bass (4 quarter notes/bar — root, 3rd, 5th, chromatic approach) and shuffle drum groove (triplet hi-hat pattern). Acoustic steel guitar leads over barrelhouse piano comping. Tension arc 0.0→0.3→0.6 adds chromatic approach notes before chord-tone downbeats across P1→P3. Call-response phrasing enforced: melody drops after beat 1 in response bars (3,4,7,8,11,12) for authentic stop-time feel.

patterns.label: Pattern 1 — intro statement
patterns.note: Clean statement. Walking bass + shuffle groove established. No approach notes.

patterns.label: Pattern 2 — groove development
patterns.note: 30% of chord-tone downbeats get chromatic approach notes (B3 before C4 etc.).

patterns.label: Pattern 3 — resolution peak
patterns.note: 60% approach note density. Full blues chromatic color at peak tension.
